body:
### Background
Prompt guidance currently defaults many reward and loot examples to gold wording, which pushes responses toward a single fantasy-currency style even when campaign tone/setting suggests different currency names.

### Goals
- Keep reward output structurally compatible with existing schema (`rewards_box.gold` remains canonical numeric field).
- Make prompt instructions explicitly require setting-appropriate currency labels in narrative/loot text.
- Apply the rule consistently across core prompt docs used in reward/planning/living-world/mechanics flows.

### Tenets
- Preserve existing state schema and field names for backward compatibility.
- Change instruction language, not runtime logic.
- Keep examples concrete while preventing hardcoded “gold-only” behavior.

### High-level description of changes
This PR updates prompt documentation to separate canonical numeric currency storage from narrative currency naming. In short: the model still writes numeric rewards into `gold`, but narrative-facing labels should match the campaign setting (for example crowns, credits, scrip, etc.) rather than always saying gold.

### Testing
- Ran targeted prompt-related tests:
  - `python3 -m pytest mvp_site/tests/test_dialog_prompt_loading.py mvp_site/tests/test_prompts.py -k "living_world_instruction or rewards" -q`
  - Result: `7 passed, 73 deselected`

### Low-level details (up to 20 files)
- **File path**: `mvp_site/prompts/rewards_system_instruction.md`
  Justification: Core rewards prompt needed an explicit currency-theming rule. Updated hard rules, rewards-box notes, and reward examples to avoid hardcoded gold wording while keeping `gold` as canonical numeric field.
- **File path**: `mvp_site/prompts/game_state_instruction.md`
  Justification: Added canonical-currency clarification where `rewards_box.gold` is defined and updated risk-reward wording to setting-appropriate currency payout.
- **File path**: `mvp_site/prompts/planning_protocol.md`
  Justification: Updated risk/reward table loot column from gold-specific bonuses to setting-appropriate currency bonuses.
- **File path**: `mvp_site/prompts/living_world_instruction.md`
  Justification: Updated quest reward hints/examples so living-world quest offers don’t default to gold-only language.
- **File path**: `mvp_site/prompts/mechanics_system_instruction.md`
  Justification: Updated starting-resource and loot guidance to use baseline/setting-appropriate currency naming.
- **File path**: `mvp_site/prompts/mechanics_system_instruction_code_execution.md`
  Justification: Mirrored the same currency-theming guidance in the code-execution variant to keep behavior aligned across prompt modes.

## Evidence
- **Terminal Recording**: [asciinema verify unit tests](https://asciinema.org/a/ciEu9JYRUlFihiQc)
- **Evidence Bundle**: Generated locally
- **Design Doc**: Generated and committed at `docs/design/pr-designs/pr-6150.md`

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Low risk: changes are limited to prompt documentation/content and associated checksum/version updates, with added tests to prevent regressions; no runtime game logic is modified.
> 
> **Overview**
> Updates multiple prompt markdowns to **stop defaulting rewards/loot text to “gold”** and instead require *setting-appropriate currency labels* (e.g., crowns/credits/scrip) while explicitly keeping `rewards_box.gold` as the canonical numeric currency field.
> 
> Refreshes prompt contract metadata (`prompt_tool_contracts.json` versions/sha256) and adds prompt-content regression tests in `test_prompts.py` to enforce consistent terminology and prevent currency amounts from leaking into loot example arrays. Also includes minor `.beads/issues.jsonl` maintenance entries.
> 
> <sup>Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit afd09a90a0897f64d31bcc89489de1bfa1ec90d2. Bugbot is set up for automated code reviews on this repo. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Replaced hardcoded "gold" with setting-appropriate currency phrasing while preserving canonical numeric reward amounts.

* **Documentation**
  * Clarified prompt guidance, examples, and loot formatting to use themed currency names and tightened reward wording.

* **Tests**
  * Added tests to validate currency terminology, loot/reward examples, and placeholder handling in prompt content.

* **Chores**
  * Updated issue records and bumped prompt contract versions.

title: prompts: make currency setting-aware (not always gold)
